title: AI Story Maker
creator: Agent Buildprint
summary: A Mapper OS v3 executable packet for building an AI story-making product with premise intake, story world graph, cast and relationship model, outline board, scene storyboard, editable generation loop, continuity review, provider boundaries, persistence, export proof, and progressive-disclosure UI requirements.
plainDescription: AI Story Maker turns seed ideas or notes into a structured, editable story package: premise, world rules, cast, relationships, outline, storyboard cards, draft scenes, continuity notes, and exportable artifacts. The packet requires honest provider blocked states, durable state, semantic output review, viewport/editor stress proof, progressive-disclosure information architecture, screen-state budgeting, and final critical review before any completion claim.
promise: A source-independent, proof-gated implementation packet for a serious creative-writing product. It does not claim live AI, deployment, story quality, export quality, or production readiness until matching evidence exists.
whatYouGet: Architecture, env, setup receipt, and local skill harness requirements in 01-project-setup.md
Dynamic UI identity generation contract in 02-ui-identity.md after setup
blueprint.yaml with central output contract and typed quality gates
Six comprehensive implementation phases plus critical-review-pushback
HANDOVER.md template that forces built/verified/blocked/not-proven reporting
whatYouNeed: A target implementation repo or empty app directory
Provider credentials only when upgrading beyond deterministic fixture proof
Browser/screenshot proof for the story-making surface
Persistence and export readback proof before durable/export claims
Security and privacy review before public deployment
risks: Building a generic writing prompt form instead of a story-making product
Stuffing intake, story world, cast, outline, storyboard, drafting, review, export, status, and help into one permanent page instead of using progressive disclosure
Applying a metaphor skin to a flat dashboard or card pile instead of defining a task flow, navigation model, and disclosure hierarchy
Shipping static outline cards that cannot be edited or regenerated
Counting deterministic fixture text as live provider or story-quality proof
Hiding missing provider, persistence, export, or deployment blockers behind optimistic success UI
Producing repeated generic story beats that do not use cast, relationships, or continuity state
Letting mobile/editor stress fail through clipped scene text, hidden controls, or page-level overflow
checks: Website sync/build after publication update when publishing

trustBadges.label: Mapper OS v3
trustBadges.detail: Phase-driven packet with YAML routing and Markdown implementation objectives.

trustBadges.label: Typed quality gates
trustBadges.detail: Viewport, editor stress, semantic output, provider/operator, and critical-review proof are selected by artifact type.

trustBadges.label: Story-output contract
trustBadges.detail: Central output quality is defined around premise, cast, relationships, beats, scenes, continuity, revision handles, and export.

trustBadges.label: Proof required
trustBadges.detail: Live provider, persistence, export, deployment, security, and story-quality claims remain blocked until evidence exists.

copyPrompt: Use the AI Story Maker Buildprint. First bootstrap exact snapshots: agb start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/ai-story-maker/package.json . If agb is not installed, clone https://github.com/DomEscobar/agent-buildprint and run node agent-buildprint/bin/agb.js start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/ai-story-maker/package.json . Then read .buildprint/next-agent.md and continue. Do not write Buildprint snapshots manually. Follow the packet read order, especially 01-project-setup.md, 02-ui-identity.md, blueprint.yaml typed_quality_gates, 03-phases/phase-flow.md, and critical-review-pushback before claiming completion. Before phase work, run agb harness init . when available so AGENTS.md and local Buildprint skills exist. Before UI code, define docs/ui-identity.md with the task-flow navigation model, disclosure hierarchy, screen-state budget, visible-now vs hidden/reachable surfaces, modal/drawer/step/route placement, and visible-together exclusions. Keep generated docs minimal: docs/ui-identity.md and docs/architecture.md only. Do not create docs/product-experience.md, docs/product-loop.md, docs/proof-matrix.md, docs/proof-strategy.md, docs/output-quality.md. Do not build a one-big-view dashboard or card pile that exposes intake, world, cast, outline, storyboard, drafting, review, export, status, and help all at once unless the product is explicitly a monitoring dashboard, which AI Story Maker is not. Compose the product temporally through focused steps or views, not spatially by stuffing every capability into the first viewport. Reject metaphor-skin compliance: a loom/canvas/board label does not pass unless the interaction model, mobile flow, and primary creative act are actually better.
